{{nihongo|'''Futakata no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|二方国造|''futakata no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''futakata kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[#Territory|Futakata Province]].

== Overview ==
=== Ancestry ===
* According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Seimu]], '''{{ill|Mineb-no-Mikoto|ja|美尼布命}}''', a grandson of {{ill|Utsurikoma-Ichinu-no-Mikoto|ja|遷狛一奴命}} who shared the same ancestor as the [[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]], was appointed Futakata no Kuni no Miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
The '''{{ill|Futakata clan|ja|二方氏}}''' (''futakata-uji''; [[kabane]] of [[Atai (kabane)|Atai]]), presumably. Wooden tablets ({{ill|mokkan|ja|木簡}}) excavated from the Heijō Palace site show individuals bearing the clan names Futakata and Futakata-be, the latter presumably being ''bemin'' administered by the former.<ref>{{ill|Suzuki Masanobu|ja|鈴木正信}}, "Part 4, Chapter 1: Kuni no Miyatsuko Clan Names and 'Kuni Names' in the Kokuzō Hongi", in ''Nihon Kodai no Shizoku to Keifu Denshō'', [[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]], 2017, pp. 371–372.</ref>

In the ''{{ill|Harima no Kuni Fudoki|ja|播磨国風土記}}'' account of a summons to the kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of [[Emperor Nintoku]], five kuni no miyatsuko — {{ill|Oki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|意岐国造|lt=Oki}}, [[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Izumo]], {{ill|Hōki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|伯岐国造|lt=Hōki}}, {{ill|Inaba no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|稲葉国造|lt=Inaba}}, and {{ill|Tajima no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|但遅麻国造|lt=Tajima}} — appear, but not the Futakata no Kuni no Miyatsuko, suggesting that its power may have been in decline.<ref>{{ill|Hōga Toshio|ja|宝賀寿男}}, "I: The Kinai Regime's Subjugation of Izumo and its Aftermath", in ''Kodai Shizoku no Kenkyū 16: The Izumo and Haji Clans — Rise and Fall of the Proto-Izumo Kingdom'', Aogaki Shuppan, 2020, p. 66.</ref><ref name="houga">Hōga Toshio, "V: Trends in Izumo Province before the Reign of [[Emperor Sujin|Sujin]]", in ''Kodai Shizoku no Kenkyū 16: The Izumo and Haji Clans'', Aogaki Shuppan, 2020, pp. 193–194.</ref>

Based on genealogies and the "Kokuzō Hongi" entry, the clan is generally regarded as kin to the {{ill|Izumo-no-Omi|ja|出雲国造|lt=Izumo-no-Omi}}.<ref name="houga" />

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base was '''Futakata-gō''' in {{ill|Futakata District|ja|二方郡}}, [[Tajima Province]].

=== Territory ===
The territory of the kuni no miyatsuko was the region then known as '''Futakata Province''', centered on the later Futakata District of Tajima Province, corresponding to most of present-day {{ill|Shin'onsen, Hyōgo|ja|新温泉町|lt=Shin'onsen-chō}} in [[Mikata District, Hyōgo|Mikata District]] and part of {{ill|Kami, Hyōgo|ja|香美町|lt=Kami-chō}} in the same district. The clan also venerated [[Kamudo Shrine (Toyooka)|Kamudo Shrine]] in Mikata-gō, {{ill|Keta District|ja|気多郡|lt=Keta District}}.<ref name="houga" />

Although [[round barrow|round kofun]] are present, no prominent [[kofun]] are known.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
'''{{ill|Futakata Shrine|ja|二方神社}}''', an {{ill|Engishiki shrine|ja|式内社|lt=shikinaisha}} of Futakata District, enshrining [[Ōnamuchi|Ōnamuji-no-Kami]].

=== Related Shrines ===
* {{ill|Ōtoshi Shrine (Shin'onsen)|ja|大歳神社 (新温泉町)|lt=Ōtoshi Shrine}} (''ōtoshi jinja'')
*: A shikinaisha enshrined in Igumi, Shin'onsen-chō. Like the kindred {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}}, it venerates [[Ōtoshi|Ōtoshi-no-Kami]].
* {{ill|Menuma Shrine|ja|面沼神社}} (''menuma jinja'')
*: A shikinaisha enshrined in Takeda, Shin'onsen-chō. It enshrines Mineb-no-Mikoto, the kuni no miyatsuko's founding ancestor.
* [[Kamudo Shrine (Toyooka)|Kamudo Shrine]] (''kamuto jinja'')
*: A shikinaisha enshrined in Hidaka-chō, [[Toyooka, Hyōgo|Toyooka City]]. It enshrines the ancestor of the Kamudo clan from which the kuni no miyatsuko descended.

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}

== See also ==
* [[San'indō]]
* [[Izumo no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

{{DEFAULTSORT:ふたかたのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:出雲氏|*ふたかたのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:但馬国]]
[[Category:兵庫県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:二方国造]]
